stimulus: In 1971, the Twenty-Sixth Amendment to the US Constitution was ratified. The amendment prohibits the federal government and states from setting a voting age higher than 18 in any election, lowering the age from the previous standard of 21. ______blank the amendment guarantees that voting-age citizens cannot be denied registration based on age in primary contests held by political parties.
stem: Which choice completes the text with the most logical transition?
rationale:
Choice B is the best answer. "Moreover" logically signals that the information in this sentence—that the amendment also guarantees that voting-age citizens cannot be denied registration in primary contests—is a separate, additional point alongside the amendment's prohibition on setting a voting age higher than 18.

Choice A is incorrect because "for instance" illogically signals that the information in this sentence exemplifies the prior point about voting-age thresholds, when in fact it presents a distinct additional requirement. Choice C is incorrect because "specifically" illogically signals that this sentence narrows the previous claim about voting-age thresholds, when it instead adds a separate guarantee about primaries. Choice D is incorrect because "instead" illogically signals that this provision is a substitute for the first, when it is actually a separate provision in addition to it.
